subject.title: Mathematics Applications & Interpretation (AI)
subject.description:
**IB Mathematics: Applications and Interpretation** is a **Group 5** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **real-world applications of mathematics, statistical analysis, mathematical modeling, and data interpretation**, with a strong focus on **technology use, practical problem-solving, and interpreting mathematical results in context**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2021**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| Assessment | Format | SL | HL | Weighting |
|------------|--------|----|----|-----------|
| **Paper 1** | External examination (calculator permitted) | 1h 30min | 2h | SL: 40%, HL: 30% |
| **Paper 2** | External examination (technology required) | 1h 30min | 2h | SL: 40%, HL: 30% |
| **Paper 3** | External examination (technology required) | N/A | 1h | HL: 20% |
| **Mathematical Exploration** | Internal assessment | 30h | 30h | 20% |

strengths: Clear logical sequence linking introduction, aim, analyses and evaluation
Detailed table of contents and numbered headings enhance navigation
Consistent use of internal sub-headings (e.g. Box Plots, PMCC, T-test)
weaknesses: Verbose personal anecdotes and duplicated formula blocks reduce conciseness
Some misplaced images and inconsistent formatting interrupt flow
Missing axis titles, units and figure captions in key graphs
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The exploration demonstrates clear organisation and mostly logical development, supported by a detailed contents page and coherent sub-sections.  However, lengthy digressions, formula redundancy, minor layout inconsistencies and incomplete figure labelling limit conciseness and prevent seamless flow, placing this criterion at band 2.

strengths: Appropriate use of key statistical terms (mean, standard deviation, PMCC, null hypothesis)
Effective use of multiple representations (tables, box-plots, scatter diagrams)
Generally clear explanations that support the analysis
weaknesses: Inconsistent and undefined notation (e.g. Greek letters, variable subscripts)
Occasional formula errors and formatting glitches interrupt clarity
Incomplete sentences and duplicated formula blocks reduce consistency
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Mathematical communication includes some correct and appropriate notation, alongside effective graphical and tabular representations.  However, inconsistent symbols, undefined terms and occasional formula mistakes hamper clarity, aligning the work with band 2 overall.

strengths: Investigation clearly driven by the studentâs personal interest in football
Justified removal of outlier and choice of statistical tests show initiative
Contextual explanations (e.g. momentum shifts) demonstrate creativity
weaknesses: Mathematical techniques remain conventional without outstanding innovation
Personal narratives sometimes overlap with standard coursework steps
Opportunity for deeper novel approaches not fully exploited
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The exploration is underpinned by genuine personal engagementâselecting an original dataset, justifying methodological choices and weaving in personal observations.  While this shows significant creativity and independent thinking, it does not reach the outstanding level, warranting band 2.

strengths: Meaningful reflection on statistical outcomes (weak r-values, p-values)
Critical decision-making evidenced in outlier removal discussion
Reflections link outcomes to learning and suggest further data collection
weaknesses: Limited depth in critique of underlying assumptions (e.g. normality for t-test)
Lacks a formal sensitivity analysis to quantify outlier impact
Reflection remains at a descriptive rather than deeply critical level
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The student provides thoughtful reflection on results and methodological choices, with clear links to understanding.  However, the analysis of limitations and alternative approaches is not substantially critical, placing this at band 2.

strengths: Relevant course-level mathematics (descriptive statistics, box-plots, PMCC, t-test) applied effectively to the research question
Interpretations of means and standard deviations are accurate and insightful
Techniques are deployed in a coherent sequence addressing the aim
weaknesses: Transcription and labelling errors in numerical results (e.g. Syy values)
Variable notation inconsistencies and absence of a regression line
Limited discussion of statistical assumptions and rigour
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Mathematics used is appropriate and effective at AI SL level, with mostly correct calculations and coherent interpretation.  Errors in transcription and labelling, along with only partial treatment of assumptions, align the work with band 3 performance.

strandSelections.reasoning: Selecting an original football dataset, justifying removal of an outlier and deciding on appropriate statistical tests demonstrate significant independent thinking, though these steps remain within standard coursework expectations rather than outstanding innovation.

The student demonstrates good understanding of each statistical tool, articulating purpose and interpreting outcomes (e.g. how p-values inform hypothesis decisions).  Misunderstandings of notation and limited discussion of underlying assumptions show the understanding is solid but not thorough or rigorous, aligning with band 3.

Most numerical results are believable and coherent, but several transcription errors (e.g. Syy stated as 515.6 yet later square-rooted as 15.6) and mis-labelled variables indicate lapses.  Overall accuracy is âmostly correctâ rather than fully correct with only minor slips.

The bulk of the writing is relevant to the aim, but the narrative is frequently verboseâlengthy personal anecdotes, duplication of formulae and extended match commentaries add little mathematically.  These digressions reduce conciseness, though they do not dominate the whole paper; hence the strand fits âmostly relevant with some unnecessary elementsâ.

Key statistical terms (mean, standard deviation, PMCC, null hypothesis) and symbolic notation are used, yet there are inconsistencies: Greek letters are sometimes undefined or mis-typed, subscripts switch forms (Fg vs Fq), and variables in formulas do not always match later commentary.  This places the work in the âsome appropriateâ rather than âmostly appropriateâ category.

Explanations are understandable, but occasional notation slips, formatting glitches and sentence fragments (e.g. duplicated quartile formulas) interrupt the flow.  Thus communication is mostly clear with some inconsistencies, aligning with band 2.

All mathematics used (descriptive statistics, box-plots, PMCC, 2-sample t-test) is solidly at AI SL level and directly relevant to the aim.  The techniques are applied in a sequence that addresses the research question, indicating effective use, though the work does not reach the sophistication or rigour of higherâlevel calculus or modelling required for bands 5-6.

The student employs tables, comparative box-plots, scatter diagrams and numerical summaries, allowing effective multiple representation of the data.  Graphs are generally labelled and support the discussion, although axis titles are basic and some image resolution is low; overall, the use is âeffectiveâ rather than âsophisticated.â

The student reflects on statistical outcomes (e.g. weak r-values, p-values > 0.05) and considers implications, giving meaningful reflection, but the depth stops short of critical, nuanced examination expected for the top band.

Including extra-time as a potential confounder and investigating the SpainâFrance outlier show initiative and some creativity, yet the mathematical techniques remain conventional; therefore the strand fits âsignificant creativity and initiativeâ rather than âoutstanding.â

The report follows a clear sequence: introduction, aim, data collection, successive analyses and a closing evaluation.  Each section builds logically on the previous one (e.g. descriptive statistics â box-plots â PMCC â t-test) which allows the reader to follow the argument without major jumps.  Although some passages are lengthy and occasionally repetitive, the central line of reasoning (comparing halves and stages) is maintained throughout, showing coherent and largely logical development.

There is basic critique of methodsâdiscussion of extra time, tactical factors and own goals influencing resultsâbut evaluation of mathematical limitations (sampling assumptions, normality for t-test) is limited.  Hence âmeaningful analysis with some critiqueâ (band 2).

Reflections are linked to the studentâs understandingâacknowledging that weak correlations undermine initial expectations and suggesting larger datasetsâdemonstrating a clear, though not âstrong,â connection to learning.

A detailed table of contents, numbered headings and separate appendix demonstrate deliberate structuring.  Introduction, body, conclusion and evaluation are easy to locate and internal sub-headings (Box Plots, PMCC, T-test) are used consistently.  Minor organisational flaws (duplicate formula blocks, some misplaced images) prevent the seamless quality expected of the top band but overall the exploration is well organised.

The exploration is clearly driven by the studentâs personal interest in football; the narrative frequently references personal observations and attendance at matches, indicating a genuine personal approach that guides the work, but it does not yet âenhance understandingâ at the highest level.

annotations.comment: Add axis titles and units to the box plot and ensure legible resolution to support the analysis visually and enhance presentation.
The title and exploration subtitle are clear and informative, effectively conveying the investigationâs focus.
The âPersonal Code:â label appears without content, interrupting the flow; consider moving or populating it appropriately or omitting it if unnecessary.
The detailed Table of Contents with clear section titles and page numbers enhances navigation and reflects deliberate organization.
Some entries use inconsistent punctuation and layout (e.g. âStatistical calculations.â vs âBox Plots.â); ensure uniform formatting throughout the contents list.
The introduction interweaves the studentâs passion for mathematics and football, demonstrating a clear personal approach that drives the investigation.
The sentence ending with âandâ in the Data Collection section is incomplete, disrupting coherence. Revise to clearly state how the data subsets will be used.
Variable notation such as $$\overline{G}_{Fg}$$ and $$\sigma_{Fg}$$ is introduced correctly but could be tabulated for clarity, ensuring consistent usage throughout.
Include a summary table defining each symbol (e.g. $$\overline{G}_{Fk}$$, $$\sigma_{Sk}$$) to avoid potential confusion when referencing formulas later.
The mean formula displays $$\overline{G}=\frac{\Sigma g}{\Sigma}$$ but uses an undefined \hat{\mathcal{U}} for sample size; replace with standard notation $$n$$ to maintain mathematical rigor.
The numerical calculations for $$\overline{G}_{Fg}$$ and $$\overline{G}_{Sg}$$ are accurate and appropriately interpret the difference in sample means.
The discussion effectively interprets the calculated standard deviations, linking numerical results to the spread and consistency of goals in each half.
When referring to Appendix Table 4, include a parenthetical reference (e.g. Table 4) to guide the reader directly to the supporting data.
The displayed formula $$\frac{G_{Fk}-\overline{G}_{Fk}}{(G_{Fk}-\overline{G}_{Fk})^2}$$ is incorrect; please revise to show the intended calculation clearly (e.g. variance or z-score).
The narrative suggests significance from descriptive statistics; consider explicitly referencing the subsequent box plots or PMCC to substantiate the claim of a âsignificant difference.â
The outlier formula uses âU.0â which is undefined; revise to standard outlier notation (e.g. $$Q3 + 1.5\,IQR$$) for clarity.
The quartile formula block is duplicated across two pages; remove redundancy to maintain conciseness and flow.
Consider including a small table listing Q1, median, and Q3 values alongside the box plot to reinforce your descriptive analysis numerically.
The investigation into why the Spain vs. France match is an outlier demonstrates critical analysis and personal engagement, enriching the exploration.
Ensure that figures are consistently numbered and captioned (e.g. Figure 1, Figure 2) to streamline cross-referencing and maintain presentation coherence.
The contextual explanation of momentum shifts using in-match statistics adds depth to the analysis and demonstrates initiative in exploring confounding variables.
The momentum figures are insightful but would benefit from accompanying numerical summaries (e.g. shot counts in text) to reinforce visual data interpretation.
The decision to remove the outlier is supported by contextual factors, demonstrating critical reflection and thoughtful consideration of data validity.
Include a brief sensitivity analysis comparing results with and without the outlier to quantify its impact on your overall conclusions.
The rationale for plotting first half goals on the horizontal axis and second half on the vertical axis is clearly stated, reflecting good mathematical communication.
Enhance the scatter diagram by adding a regression line to visualize the trend and support subsequent PMCC calculations more effectively.
